qualifying_org_definition: Community-based organizations, workforce programs, training providers; volunteer/community service work explicitly listed as qualifying alongside employment and approved programs. Partner sign-up to appear on DSS resource list is encouraged but not a prerequisite for hours to count.
verification_instrument: SNAP ABAWD Work Activity Timesheet (English and Spanish, updated 03/27/2026); alternative documentation (letter or custom timesheet with participant name, program/activity, month/year, dates/hours, description, and supervisor signature) is also accepted
hours_formula_detail: Nevada follows the federal 80-hour/month baseline; Partner Toolkit Quick Reference states explicitly 'Participants must complete 80 hours per month of qualifying activities (unless exempt).'
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Nevada source addresses whether the certifying nonprofit must be in-state. The DSS ABAWD Work Activity Timesheet (2938-EF 02/26) lists qualifying unpaid work as 'Volunteering at state, local, religious, or community non-profit organizations' with no geographic restriction. The partner toolkit's sign-up process is voluntary (encouraged, not required). Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) permits any qualifying nonprofit; absent an explicit Nevada restriction, out-of-state nonprofits should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: No active statewide waiver as of 2026. Nevada held a statewide ABAWD time-limit waiver approved Feb 1, 2025 through Jan 31, 2026 (USDA FNS partial approval, Mar 14, 2025). That waiver expired Jan 31, 2026. Nevada's OBBBA-era abawd_enforced_2026 status is 'yes' — fully enforced statewide with no current county-level or statewide waiver in effect.
submission_deadline: unknown — no explicit deadline published in available DSS sources; the timesheet captures a single 'Reporting Month & Year' implying monthly submission concurrent with or following the reporting month
notes:
Nevada is Tier 1 clean: the ABAWD Partner Toolkit (Feb 2026) confirms that community service and volunteer work explicitly count toward the 80-hour monthly requirement, verified by a state Work Activity Timesheet signed by an org supervisor. Community partner sign-up to appear on the DSS statewide resource list is encouraged but is not a mandatory prerequisite for hours to count — the toolkit states organizations 'are encouraged to report updates promptly.' No EDP, county assignment, MOU, or provider registration is required before volunteer hours are creditable. Remote work stance not addressed in available sources.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial check conducted to try to refute the Tier 1 rating. Four targeted searches run: (1) NV SNAP ABAWD work registration enrollment intake requirement 2026; (2) NV SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 volunteer community service track; (3) NV SNAP ABAWD approved volunteer site MOU nonprofit registration requirement; (4) dss.nv.gov ABAWD partner sign-up mandatory volunteer hours qualify. The Nevada SNAPET FY2026 State Plan PDF (83 pages) and the ABAWD Community Partner Toolkit PDF (7 pages, including Quick Reference and FAQs) were extracted directly via pdfplumber and read in full. Key findings: (a) The ABAWD FAQs (embedded in the partner toolkit, pages 6-7) list 'community service or volunteer work' as a standalone qualifying activity with no SNAPET enrollment requirement — it is listed on equal footing with employment and approved work programs. (b) The SNAPET FY2026 State Plan explicitly states 'Nevada is a voluntary state; all work registrants are exempt from mandatory E&T participation' (page 17, estimated participants exempt = 100%). SNAPET is opt-in; it is not a required prestep. (c) The partner toolkit's sign-up section says organizations 'are encouraged' to register and 'are encouraged to report updates promptly' — permissive language, not a condition of crediting hours. No mandatory registration, MOU, or formal agreement language found anywhere in either document. (d) Hour verification is self-reported by participant via a DSS timesheet or equivalent letter with supervisor signature — no case manager determination of hours. (e) The SNAPET State Plan describes a SNAPET Employment Plan process, but this is only for individuals who voluntarily enroll in SNAPET, not for participants using the general volunteer/community-service lane. No evidence of any hidden prestep, gating mechanism, or mandatory org registration found. Tier 1 clean classification is confirmed with high confidence, now backed by direct extraction of primary-source state documents.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: DSS ABAWD FAQ and Partner Toolkit Quick Reference both explicitly state '80 hours per month' — federal_80 applies. The USDA FNS waiver approval letter (Mar 14, 2025) also cites '80 hours a month' as the ABAWD work requirement. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: No Nevada policy source addresses geographic restrictions on certifying nonprofits. The official timesheet's activity description lists 'Volunteering at state, local, religious, or community non-profit organizations' with no in-state requirement. Federal default applies (any qualifying nonprofit). Coded 'silent.' (3) Geographic waivers: Nevada's last statewide ABAWD time-limit waiver (partial approval Feb 1, 2025–Jan 31, 2026) has expired. As of 2026-06-21, no active waiver is in effect and ABAWD is fully enforced statewide. Source: USDA FNS nv-abawd-response-fy2025.pdf and fna.usda.gov/snap/abawd/waivers/2025-2029. (4) Submission UX: The DSS website provides a PDF timesheet with no online portal for submission; submission to the local DSS office (paper/in-person) is the implied mechanism. The timesheet is monthly (captures 'Reporting Month & Year'). No specific submission deadline is published. (5) Form fields: Extracted directly from 2938-EF (02/26) timesheet PDF: requires participant name, Case ID/UPI, reporting month/year, org/supervisor name, activity type (description), hours by week, total hours, supervisor name/signature/phone, and participant attestation signature. Mapped to schema fields accordingly.
